content: Hey, I'm feeling really overwhelmed with my job as a Real Estate Agent in Austin, TX. I've been thinking about making a change, but I'm not sure what to do.
I understand how you feel. What specifically is making you feel overwhelmed about your job? Is it the long hours or the stress of dealing with clients?

It's a bit of both, to be honest. I've been working non-stop for the past 5 years and I feel like I'm burning out. I've also been taking care of my aging parents, which has added an extra layer of stress to my life.
That sounds really tough. Have you considered taking a break or finding ways to manage your stress better? Maybe we can brainstorm some ideas together.
I've actually been thinking about moving to Denver, CO to be closer to my parents. I've heard the real estate market is really booming there and I think it could be a great opportunity for me.
That's a big decision! What do you think you'll miss most about Austin if you were to move? And have you thought about how you'll balance your work and family responsibilities in a new city?
I'll definitely miss the food scene here - I love cooking Thai food and trying out new recipes. But I think the benefits of being closer to my parents will outweigh the costs.
It's great that you're prioritizing your family. What kind of support system do you have in place for your parents currently? And how do you think your job will change if you were to move to Denver?

I currently have a caregiver who comes in a few times a week to help out, but I think it would be better for me to be there in person. As for my job, I'm hoping to find a similar position in Denver and maybe even start my own real estate firm one day.
That's a great goal to have! Starting your own business can be a challenging but rewarding experience. What kind of timeline are you thinking of for making this move and starting your own firm?
I'm thinking of making the move within the next 6-12 months. I want to make sure I have everything in place before I take the leap.
That sounds like a solid plan. What do you think you'll need to do to prepare for this big change? Maybe we can make a list together.
I'll need to start looking for a new place to live, and also start networking in the Denver real estate scene. I'd also like to start saving up more money to cover any unexpected expenses.

Those are all great steps to take. I'd also recommend reaching out to your professional network and letting them know about your plans to move. You never know who might be able to introduce you to someone in the Denver area.

That's a great idea. I'll make sure to reach out to my colleagues and friends and let them know what's going on. I'm also thinking of starting a travel blog to document my experiences in Denver and share them with others.
A travel blog sounds like a great way to share your experiences and connect with others who may be interested in the same things. What kind of content are you thinking of featuring on your blog?
I'm thinking of featuring articles about the best places to eat and visit in Denver, as well as some of my own personal stories about adjusting to life in a new city.

That sounds like a great plan. I'm excited to see how your blog turns out! Remember to take care of yourself during this big transition and don't hesitate to reach out if you need any further guidance or support.
